pattern: Captions must be positioned in 'safe zones' to avoid obscuring app UI elements (buttons, brand names) or other critical on-screen information.
Text styling (color, highlights, background) must ensure high contrast and readability against all backgrounds, avoiding visual clashes with other on-screen elements.
All brand assets, especially logos, must be fully visible and not cropped or obscured throughout the ad.
Recording artifacts (e.g., screen recording UI, watermarks) reduce professionalism and should be removed from final footage.
Captions should not overlap or be obscured by other on-screen visual elements.
Ensure caption readability by using sufficient contrast with the background, potentially with a text background or scrim.
Keep captions concise for quick readability, especially in fast-scrolling environments.
Never feature competitor logos, UI, or products in ads.
Captions require sufficient contrast against their background for readability, especially on busy or bright frames.
All ad visual elements, including background colors, must adhere to brand guidelines.
Avoid dominant off-brand colors in backgrounds.
Ensure captions and other text do not collide with app UI elements.
Avoid prominent off-brand colors in on-screen clothing or props.
Maintain consistent application of brand-specific colors (e.g., highlights) across all frames.
Captions should be positioned in clear spaces to avoid overlapping critical UI elements (e.g., app UI).
When displaying phone screens or app interfaces, ensure the entire screen is visible and not cropped.
Caption text must have sufficient contrast against its background to ensure readability.
Ensure all visual elements, including character attire, adhere to the approved brand color palette.
Captions must be positioned to avoid collision with any background UI elements or text within the footage.
Always use the official and approved brand tagline, especially in final branding elements like outros.
